findings.evidence: README:372-390 layer-3 build list has no normal subgroup/Diff₀/conjugation; grep of roadmap finds no 'Diff₀'/'isotopic-to-id' target (only knot-equivalence reference notes). grep of code/TauCeti shows finalHomeomorph/conj used only in Isotopy.lean and AmbientIsotopyConj.lean — no downstream consumer. Description: 'the standalone conjugation-closure prerequisite for the normal subgroup of homeomorphisms isotopic to the identity'.
findings.fix: Hold until a human adds the mapping-class / isotopic-to-identity-normal-subgroup direction (and its conjugation prerequisite) to GeometricTopology/README.md as a named target, or re-justify conj against a specific named layer-2/3/6 consumer with a visible path.
findings.issue: conj/finalHomeomorph_conj sit on no proximate path to a named roadmap target. The PR's own description scopes it as the prerequisite for 'the normal subgroup of homeomorphisms isotopic to the identity' (target id 'isotopic-to-id-normal-subgroup'), which is not a roadmap node and was blocked previously. Layer 3's build list (Diff(M) group, C^∞ topology, smooth-families map, O(n+1)→Diff(Sⁿ)) excludes normal subgroups, Diff₀, and conjugation; the convention's named consumers (layers 2/3/6) do not visibly need conjugation of an ambient isotopy by a homeomorphism. This is a prerequisite-of-a-prerequisite for the same blocked analogy, now one hop removed.

summary: Single-topic is fine, but roadmap fit fails: this narrows to the conjugation operation conj whose only stated and visible purpose (per the description) is the normal subgroup of homeomorphisms isotopic to the identity — a target the prior review blocked and that no human has added to the roadmap. No named layer (layer 3's explicit build list excludes normal subgroups, Diff₀, and conjugation) proximately needs conjugation of ambient isotopies, and there is no downstream consumer in the tree.
